OUTBUILDING BLOWN ON ROAD.
NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN.
SEMI TRUCK OVERTURNED ON U.S. 231 JUST NORTH OF PHOENIX DRIVE DUE TO STRONG WINDS

REPORT RELAYED FROM JKL NWS. ROOF TAKEN OFF BLDG. BARK OFF TELEPHONE POLES. DOOR TO GARAGE RIPPED OFF AN THROWN AROUND METAL POLE. DAMAGE LOCATED A MILE OR TWO WEST OF CLIFTY FALLS STATE PARKS NORTH ENTRANCE.

EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT MADISON COUNTY AIRPORT. DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO IMS LN. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 0.3 MILES LONG AND 60 YARDS WIDE. A LARGE DUMPSTER WAS MOVED 35 FEET AND AN AIRPLANE WAS SHIFTED. PART OF A HANGARS SIDING CAME OFF ALONG WITH A DISABLED AWNING AND POST AT THE AIRPORT OPERATIONS BUILDING. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE NARROW PATH.
MAJOR BUILDING DAMAGE...ONE BUILDING LOST ROOF ON IN 62. POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN. WIND DAMAGE A MILE WIDE NEAR AIRPORT.
DAMAGE TO BUILDING AT AIRPORT

EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON EAST RILEY RD IN FLOYDS KNOBS AND DESTROYED A GARAGE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST ALONG BINFORD ROAD WHERE MULTIPLE TREES WERE DOWN. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST ACROSS PAOLI PIKE AND CAUSED ROOF DAMAGE AT KNOB POINT APARTMENTS BEFORE LIFTING.

EF-0 TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN AT THE WAL-MART PARKING LOT AT VETERANS PARKWAY NEAR I-65. A VEHICLE WAS TURNED OVER ON I-65. THE TORNADO SKIPPED TO A SUBDIVISION EAST OF THERE CAUSING DAMAGE ALONG BELMAR AND MEADOWS DR AS WELL AS AT THE INTERSECTION OF KINGSFIELD ST AND CROWN CT. IT TOOK DOWN SEVERAL FENCES AND THEN LATER THE ROOF OF A BARN ON ARMSTRONG RD... WHERE THE TORNADO ENDED.
4 LARGE UTILITY POLES BROKEN ON DAKOTA AVE AND FLOYD ST.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR CLOVERPORT
TWITTER REPORT OF POWER LINES DOWN AT WATTERSON AND BROWNSBORO RD.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEED ESTIMATED AT 95 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 4.2 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR INTERSECTION OF I-264 AND BROWNSBORO ROAD AND TRACKED NORTHEAST TO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HURSTBOURNE AND BROWNSBORO WHERE IT LIFTED BRIEFLY. IT THEN REFORMED AND CROSSED I-265.
TREE FELL ON HOUSE IN THE 3600 BLOCK OF REMS RD
BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED REPORT OF ONE POSSIBLY TWO SEMIS OVERTURNED NEAR BROWNSBORO EXIT ON SNYDER.
TREES DOWN ONE ON TOP OF TRUCK IN VICINITY OF GOOSE CREEK AND HWY 22. SIDE OF HOUSE BLOWN OFF ALONG BAY WINDOW AND SHINGLE DAMAGE.
AIR CONDITIONING UNIT BLOWN OFF DOWNTOWN HOTEL.

SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND SEVERAL SNAPPED ALONG WITH SHINGLES BLOWN OFF HOUSES ON STONEY BROOK DR NEAR MICHAEL EDWARDS DR. A TREE WAS TOPPED NEAR ROMAN CT. THE ROOF OF ONE HOUSE WAS SHIFTED 4-6 INCHES. SOME SIDING TAKEN OFF OF HOUSES AS WELL.

ADDITIONAL ISOLATED WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE WINDY WILLOW AND ORCHARD GRASS HILLS SUBDIVISION. SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED CLOSE TO THE GROUND ALONG WITH MINOR SIDING AND ROOF ISSUES ON AREA HOUSES.
CONTINUATION OF JEFFERSON COUNTY TORNADO INTO OLDHAM COUNTY. TRACK LENGTH 1.8 MILES IN OLDHAM COUNTY THROUGH ORCHARD GRASS HILLS...ENDING AT THE NORTHERN TIP OF CLORE LANE.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 95-100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN JUST TO THE NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF STONY BROOK DR AND HURSTBOURNE PKWY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED TO THE NNE FOR 0.45 MILES AND LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LAVERNE DR AND STONY BROOK. THE PATH WIDTH WAS 90 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. SIDING AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF HOUSES. A GARAGE HAD ITS DOOR BLOWN IN AND ITS ROOF LIFTED AND SHIFTED ONE FOOT.

CITY EMERGANCY MANAGEMENT REPORTED TWO ROADS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. THEY WERE BLUFF BOONE AND GOOSE CREEK ROADS. RADAR ESTIMATED THAT AROUND 2 INCHES FELL WITHIN 2 HOURS.

ONE INCH HAIL REPORTED ALONG STATE HIGHWAY 127 5 MILES NORTHWEST OF RUSSELL SPRINGS. ALSO...DEBRIS WAS REPORTED. RADAR INDICATED A VERY STRONG CIRCULATION.

EF-2 TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF HIGHWAY 127 IN NORTHERN RUSSELL COUNTY. TRACKED NORTHEAST INTO SOUTHERN CASEY COUNTY AND LIFTED WEST OF THE TOWN OF WINDSOR. THE WORST DAMAGED OCCURRED IN NORTHERN RUSSELL COUNTY EAST OF HIGHWAY 127 WHERE IT DESTROYED TWO MOBILE HOMES AND ROTATED A MODULAR HOME.

EF-2 TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HIGHWAY 127 AND MOUNT UNION ROAD. TRACKED NORTHEAST INTO SOUTHERN CASEY COUNTY AND LIFTED NEAR THE TOWN OF WINDSOR. OUTBUILDINGS ALONG WITH BARNS AND A MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED. TORNADO STRENGTH DECREASED TO A EF-1 IN CASEY COUNTY.

THE TORNADO FROM RUSSELL COUNTY CONTINUED INTO SOUTHERN CASEY COUNTY. IT WEAKENED TO EF-1 STRENGTH IN CASEY COUNTY DAMAGING TREES...BARNS...AND OUTBUILDINGS BEFORE IT LIFTED 2.3 MILES WSW OF WINDSOR. TOTAL TRACK LENGTH WAS 7.2 MILES ACROSS NORTHERN RUSSELL AND SOUTHERN CASEY COUNTIES.
